raw_consumer_statement:
I was in XXXX XXXX from XXXX XXXX, and made only ONE transaction with my Barclay 's card during that time, and that was for a taxi ride from downtown to my hotel, which should have been in the XXXX XXXXXXXX {$7.00} range. When I returned home, a transaction for {$520.00} appeared on my statement for a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. At no time during my visit did I purchase anything from a clothing store, as the transaction indicates, nor was I ever near the merchant location. The documentation I received from Barclay 's shows the merchant transaction, but not the itemized list of what supposedly was purchased and the signature was not mine. Also, there is no transaction for a taxi ride on my statement. What I believe happened was the taxi driver who took my card charged {$520.00} using the clothing store 's account. Nothing else explains this. I was not given a receipt for the taxi ride, and was made to sign on a small, blank tablet ( no transaction amount was visible ) separate from the phone which the taxi driver was using to generate the transaction. I believe this is a clear case of fraud. 

Barclay 's insists they are not liable since the merchant did not violate the terms and conditions of the agreement, and by giving the taxi driver my card, I was agreeing to pay whatever amount was charged.
